symbols.name: next
symbols.doc:
next returns the next entry and true, or the saved draft at the newest entry.
It returns false once the draft is reached.

symbols.name: prev
symbols.doc:
prev returns the previous entry and true, saving current as the draft when
leaving it. It returns false at the oldest entry.

symbols.name: record
symbols.doc:
record stores input unless it is blank or repeats the newest entry. It resets
navigation and persists accepted entries.

symbols.name: Error
symbols.doc: Error returns the message.

symbols.name: ExitCode
symbols.doc: ExitCode returns the exit code.

symbols.name: Unwrap
symbols.doc: Unwrap returns the wrapped error.

symbols.name: Run
symbols.doc: Run executes the eval subcommand.

symbols.name: Run
symbols.doc: Run executes the namespace subcommand.

symbols.name: Run
symbols.doc: Run executes the version subcommand.

symbols.name: IsTerminalWriter
symbols.doc:
IsTerminalWriter implements the [log.TerminalWriter] interface, which allows
the [repl] to be used as a log handler output.

symbols.name: Update
symbols.doc:
Update is the REPL's central message dispatcher. It is intentionally a thin
table mapping each message type to the method that handles it -- read it
top-to-bottom as a table of contents; the substantive logic for each
concern lives in the file named alongside its case below.

A few cases are trivial enough (2-4 lines, no shared state with other
cases) that extracting a named method would add indirection without
clarity benefit, so they remain inline: tea.WindowSizeMsg,
tea.BackgroundColorMsg, faultMsg and quitMsg.

symbols.name: View
symbols.doc:
View renders the REPL. See output.go for transcriptView/altScreenView,
which hold the two rendering modes' logic.

symbols.name: Write
symbols.doc:
Write implements the [io.Writer] interface, which allows the [repl] to be
used as a log handler output.

Log lines are queued onto logq and relayed to the running [tea.Program], in
order, by a single background goroutine (drainLog, started once by
handleReady) instead of spawning a new goroutine per call. Write blocks the
calling goroutine once the queue is full (bounded backpressure) or l.ctx is
done, whichever comes first.

symbols.name: altScreenView
symbols.doc:
altScreenView renders alt-screen mode: a scrollable output region on top,
with the active editor pinned to the bottom.

symbols.name: appendOutput
symbols.doc:
appendOutput appends text (split into lines) to the REPL's output buffer
and refreshes the viewport content.

The buffer's joined string representation is cached in bufferText and
updated incrementally here (append the delta instead of rejoining the
entire buffer), since this runs once per log line/eval output in alt-screen
mode. The full string is only rebuilt when maxOutputLines forces the front
of the buffer to be trimmed, which is bounded and infrequent.

symbols.name: drainLog
symbols.doc:
drainLog relays log lines queued by Write to the running [tea.Program] one
at a time, preserving arrival order. It exits once l.ctx is done (real
program shutdown, or automatic test-context cancellation), so no manual
cleanup is required by callers or tests.

symbols.name: evaluate
symbols.doc:
evaluate feeds input to the REPL's AST and returns its resulting string
representation. It is defined with a value receiver for immutability.

symbols.name: handleKeyPress
symbols.doc:
handleKeyPress routes a key press to the action bound to it (see keyMap),
forwarding the key to the active text editor when it isn't consumed by any
bound action.

NOTE: this is the original case body from Update's tea.KeyPressMsg case,
which fell through to a shared tail (optionally forward to the editor, then
syncViewportSize) rather than returning early. That tail is now reproduced
explicitly at the end of this method.

symbols.name: handleLog
symbols.doc:
handleLog routes a queued log line (see logsink.go) to either the output
buffer (alt-screen mode) or directly to the terminal via tea.Println.

symbols.name: handleMouseWheel
symbols.doc:
handleMouseWheel forwards wheel scroll events to the output viewport when
alt-screen mode is active.

NOTE: this case fell through to Update's shared tail (syncViewportSize)
rather than returning early; that is reproduced explicitly here.

symbols.name: handleReady
symbols.doc:
handleReady focuses the active editor, wires up the REPL as the terminal
log destination (onReady), and starts the drainLog goroutine exactly once
(guarded by logOnce -- readyMsg can fire more than once, e.g. on every
terminal resize).

NOTE: this case fell through to Update's shared tail (syncViewportSize)
rather than returning early; that is reproduced explicitly here.

symbols.name: handleSetEditMode
symbols.doc:
handleSetEditMode applies a mode switch requested via setEditMode (e.g. by
the toggle key, or by evalArea when switching from line to area mode), then
replays any queued follow-up messages -- typically the key press that
triggered the switch, so it can be reprocessed by the newly active editor.

symbols.name: onReady
symbols.doc:
onReady registers the [repl] as the output destination for terminal log
handlers, so log output is routed through the REPL's own message loop
instead of writing directly to the terminal (which would corrupt the TUI).

symbols.name: outputRegionView
symbols.doc:
outputRegionView renders the currently visible slice of the output
viewport's content.

symbols.name: syncViewportSize
symbols.doc:
syncViewportSize resizes the output viewport to fill the space not
occupied by the active editor.

symbols.name: transcriptView
symbols.doc:
transcriptView renders the plain (non-alt-screen) mode: only the active
editor is drawn; previously evaluated input/output are written directly to
the terminal's natural scrollback via tea.Println (see pipeline.go).

symbols.name: WriteTo
symbols.doc: WriteTo implements the [io.WriterTo] interface on [sourceDef].

symbols.name: Error
symbols.doc: Error pairs an error with an exit code.

symbols.name: Eval
symbols.doc: Eval is the eval subcommand.

symbols.name: Namespace
symbols.doc:
Namespace is the environment subcommand. It takes a namespace name; extra
args pass to the generator.

symbols.name: Run
symbols.doc: Run parses the command line and runs the selected subcommand.

symbols.name: Version
symbols.doc: Version is the version subcommand. All flags are mutually exclusive.

Executing REPL input requires 4 render cycles to synchronize the persistent
models and output stream without visual artifacts.

symbols.name: clamp
symbols.doc: clamp constrains v to the inclusive range [lo, hi].

symbols.name: cmdSlice
symbols.doc:
cmdSlice unwraps tea.Batch and tea.Sequence messages, both of which are []Cmd
under the hood (Sequence's message type is unexported, so reflection is used).

symbols.name: collectMsg
symbols.doc:
Executing REPL input requires 4 render cycles to synchronize the persistent
models and output stream without visual artifacts.

symbols.name: commitMsg
symbols.doc:
Executing REPL input requires 4 render cycles to synchronize the persistent
models and output stream without visual artifacts.

symbols.name: errf
symbols.doc: errf is a helper that appends a formatted message to a wrapped error.
symbols.signature: func errf(err error, template string, args ...any) (error)

symbols.name: evaluateMsg
symbols.doc:
Executing REPL input requires 4 render cycles to synchronize the persistent
models and output stream without visual artifacts.

symbols.name: history
symbols.doc:
history records evaluated inputs and recalls them by navigation.

index points one past the newest entry while the user edits a draft; prev and
next move it. An empty path keeps history in memory only.

struct_fields.name: Source
struct_fields.tag: `name:"source" short:"f" help:"Source namespace definitions exclusively from file(s)." placeholder:"file" type:"existingfile" sep:","`

symbols.name: joinInput
symbols.doc:
joinInput flattens one or more lines of value into a single space-delimited
line, suitable for display in a single-line editor.

symbols.name: keyMap
symbols.doc:
keyMap holds all key bindings recognized by the REPL. See handleKeyPress
for how each binding is routed to an action.

symbols.name: lineCount
symbols.doc: lineCount returns the number of newline-delimited lines in text.

symbols.name: loadHistory
symbols.doc:
loadHistory reads entries from path. A missing or unreadable file yields an
empty history. An empty path keeps history in memory only.

struct_fields.name: Log
struct_fields.tag: `help:"Add or modify a log handler (repeatable)." placeholder:"${logHandlerSyntax}" sep:"none"`

symbols.name: logHandlerSpec
symbols.doc:
logHandlerSpec represents a log handler specified via command-line flags.

Log handlers are used to filter, format, and route log messages.
Each message is routed to all handlers of sufficient severity level, and
each handler formats the message for its output.

If no handlers are specified, the default handler is "stdout,text,warn".

All fields of a handler specification are optional, and delimiters are only
required if a trailing field is specified. The default format and level are
output-specific.

The default log level is "info" for user-added handlers, "warn" by default.
The default log format is "text" for terminals, "json" otherwise.

Handlers specified with the same output will be merged by overriding previous
fields with later non-empty fields, and modifying the default handler automatically
promotes its level to info.

For example, ",json" updates the default handler to "stdout,json,info".

symbols.name: logMsg
symbols.doc:
logMsg is used to queue a message written to the REPL output stream.

This is required for synchronizing I/O with the REPL whether alt-screen mode
is enabled or not.

symbols.name: logQueueSize
symbols.doc:
logQueueSize bounds the number of in-flight log lines waiting to be
delivered to the running [tea.Program]. Write (see logsink.go) blocks the
calling goroutine once this buffer is full (backpressure) rather than
spawning an unbounded goroutine per log line.

symbols.name: makeExplicitSource
symbols.signature: func makeExplicitSource(path string, idx, sum int) (sourceDef)

symbols.name: makeLogMsg
symbols.doc:
makeLogMsg is a helper for constructing logMsg with optional printf-style
formatting.

It replaces noisy struct and slice literals at call sites with concise
function call syntax.

symbols.name: makeREPL
symbols.doc:
makeREPL creates a new [repl] with default settings, except for those
overridden by any provided [option].

symbols.name: msgAttr
symbols.doc:
msgAttr groups per-message-type structured log fields under a key derived
from the message's Go type, keeping log entries traceable to the exact
Update case that produced them.

symbols.name: newREPL
symbols.doc:
newREPL builds a sized, focused repl ready to accept input. It runs Init for
coverage of the model lifecycle, applies an initial window size, and focuses
the editor directly to avoid the global logger side effects of the ready
message path (covered separately by the onReady test).

symbols.name: outputWidthMax
symbols.doc: you're gonna see some serious shit

symbols.name: pump
symbols.doc:
pump executes cmd and every command it transitively produces, threading the
model through each resulting message until the program settles. Batch and
Sequence messages are unwrapped into their component commands. It deliberately
never forwards blink/tick messages into the editor (repl.Update only forwards
to the editor on key presses), so no command blocks on a timer.

symbols.name: repl
symbols.doc:
repl is the REPL's [tea.Model].

Its behavior is implemented across several files, grouped by concern:
  - repl.go: model definition, lifecycle (Init/Update dispatch), construction.
  - keyrouter.go: key bindings and key-press routing/actions.
  - pipeline.go: the collect/capture/commit/evaluate/reset eval cycle.
  - output.go: the output buffer/viewport and View rendering.
  - logsink.go: wiring the REPL as the destination for terminal log output.

symbols.name: resolveLogWriter
symbols.signature: func resolveLogWriter(output string) (io.Writer, io.Closer, bool, error)

symbols.name: runeCount
symbols.doc: runeCount returns the number of runes in s.

symbols.name: send
symbols.doc:
send applies msg to the model and then drives every command it produces to
completion, returning the settled model.
symbols.signature: func send(t *testing.T, m repl, msg tea.Msg) (repl)

symbols.name: splitInput
symbols.doc: splitInput splits value into its newline-delimited lines.

symbols.name: visible
symbols.doc: visible strips ANSI control sequences so rendered text can be asserted on.

symbols.name: waitForFocus
symbols.doc:
waitForFocus gives the program time to process the async ready cmd
triggered by tea.WindowSizeMsg (see repl.go's WindowSizeMsg case), which
focuses the editor. Sending keys before this round trip completes would
have them silently dropped by the unfocused textinput/textarea.

symbols.name: withExitCode

withExitCode wraps an error with an exit code,
or returns nil if the error is nil.

symbols.name: parse
symbols.doc: parse reads source from r and appends its position state to the AST.

symbols.name: Add
symbols.doc:
Add returns a new Pos that is the field-wise sum of the receiver p and the
given Pos q; i.e., p + q.

symbols.name: IsZero
symbols.doc:
IsZero returns whether the position is the invalid zero value.

The first byte in a stream will be at offset=0 and line=column=1.

symbols.name: String
symbols.doc:
String returns a human-readable position string, e.g. "3:15+42",
meaning line 3, column 15. The +42 byte offset refers to the same position.

symbols.name: Sub
symbols.doc:
Sub returns a new Pos that is the field-wise difference of the receiver p
and the provided Pos q; i.e., p - q.

symbols.name: AST
symbols.doc: AST represents the abstract syntax tree of a source.

symbols.name: ContextualParseError
symbols.signature: func ContextualParseError(err error, pos Pos, r io.Reader) (error)

symbols.name: Pos
symbols.doc:
Pos refers to an absolute position in a byte stream in terms of byte offset
and line:column, relative to the start of the stream.

The offset is 0-based, while the line and column are 1-based.
The first byte in a stream will be at offset=0 and line=column=1.
This is distinguished from the zero value, which is an invalid position.

symbols.name: buildContext
symbols.signature: func buildContext(line string, pos Pos, width int64) (string)

symbols.name: AddHandlers
symbols.doc:
AddHandlers adds a [Handler] built from each of the given [HandlerOptions].
It returns the first error from building a handler, if any, without adding
remaining handlers.

symbols.name: Debug
symbols.doc:
Debug emits a record at LevelDebug. Parts join with a space.
Terminal text adds the call site.

symbols.name: Debugf
symbols.doc:
Debugf emits a record at LevelDebug, formatted by fmt.Sprintf.
Terminal text adds the call site.

symbols.name: Error
symbols.doc: Error emits a record at LevelError. Parts join with a space.

symbols.name: Errorf
symbols.doc: Errorf emits a record at LevelError, formatted by fmt.Sprintf.

symbols.name: Handlers
symbols.doc: Handlers iterates a snapshot of the driver's handlers.

symbols.name: Info
symbols.doc: Info emits a record at LevelInfo. Parts join with a space.

symbols.name: Infof
symbols.doc: Infof emits a record at LevelInfo, formatted by fmt.Sprintf.

symbols.name: Log
symbols.doc: Log emits a record at level. Parts join with a space.

symbols.name: Logf
symbols.doc: Logf emits a record at level, formatted by fmt.Sprintf.
symbols.signature: func (*Driver) Logf(level Level, attrs []slog.Attr, format string, args ...any)

symbols.name: MapHandlers
symbols.doc:
MapHandlers applies fn to each [Handler] predicated by want.
It returns the first error returned by fn, if any, without evaluating fn on
remaining handlers.

It is safe to call [Handler] methods within fn, but calling [Driver] methods
is undefined behavior and may cause a deadlock.

symbols.name: RemoveHandlers
symbols.doc:
RemoveHandlers removes each given [Handler], or it removes all when called
with no arguments, and returns whether any [Handler] was removed.

symbols.name: Trace
symbols.doc:
Trace emits a record at LevelTrace. Parts join with a space.
Terminal text adds the call site.

symbols.name: Tracef
symbols.doc:
Tracef emits a record at LevelTrace, formatted by fmt.Sprintf.
Terminal text adds the call site.

symbols.name: Warn
symbols.doc: Warn emits a record at LevelWarn. Parts join with a space.

symbols.name: Warnf
symbols.doc: Warnf emits a record at LevelWarn, formatted by fmt.Sprintf.

symbols.name: Disable
symbols.doc: Disable stops the handler from receiving records.

symbols.name: Enable
symbols.doc: Enable resumes a disabled handler.

symbols.name: Enabled
symbols.doc: Enabled reports whether the handler receives records.

symbols.name: Format
symbols.doc:
Format returns the handler's output encoding. The second result is false if
the handler is uninitialized.

symbols.name: IsTerminal
symbols.doc:
IsTerminal returns whether the handler writes to a terminal.
It also returns false if the handler is uninitialized.

symbols.name: Level
symbols.doc:
Level returns the handler's highest forwarded level. The second result is
false if the handler is uninitialized.

symbols.name: Options
symbols.doc:
Options returns the handler's configuration. The second result is false if
the handler is uninitialized.

symbols.name: SetFormat
symbols.doc: SetFormat replaces the output encoding. It errors on an invalid format.

symbols.name: SetLevel
symbols.doc: SetLevel sets the highest level forwarded. It errors on an invalid level.

symbols.name: SetWriter
symbols.doc: SetWriter replaces the output. It errors on a nil writer.

symbols.name: Writer
symbols.doc:
Writer returns the handler's output. The second result is false if the
handler is uninitialized.

symbols.name: UnmarshalText
symbols.doc: UnmarshalText implements the encoding.TextUnmarshaler interface.

symbols.name: Valid

symbols.name: Allows

Allows reports whether a handler at this level forwards a record at the given
level.

symbols.name: Symbol
symbols.doc: Symbol returns the level's terminal badge.

symbols.name: Valid
symbols.doc: Valid reports whether the level is recognized.

symbols.name: AddHandlers
symbols.doc: AddHandlers calls [Driver.AddHandlers] on the package-level driver.

symbols.name: Attrs
symbols.doc:
Attrs constructs a slice of slog.Attr from alternating keys and values.

Keys that cannot be asserted as strings are replaced with a generic
identifier "{!key[%d]}" containing its argument index.

Values are constructed with [slog.Any] for deriving the type, which provides
maximum support for types handled natively by [slog.Handler]s.

If an odd number of arguments is provided, the final key is silently ignored.
Pairs with nil values are also sildently ignored.

symbols.name: Debug
symbols.doc: Debug calls Debug on the package-level driver.

symbols.name: Debugf
symbols.doc: Debugf calls Debugf on the package-level driver.

symbols.name: Default
symbols.doc:
Default returns the package driver. It is a no-op until a handler is added or
SetDefault is called.

symbols.name: Driver
symbols.doc: Driver sends each record to its handlers.

symbols.name: Error
symbols.doc: Error calls Error on the package-level driver.

symbols.name: Errorf
symbols.doc: Errorf calls Errorf on the package-level driver.

symbols.name: Format
symbols.doc: Format is a Handler output encoding. The zero value is invalid.

symbols.name: FormatRange
symbols.doc: FormatRange returns the lowest and highest valid formats.

symbols.name: Group
symbols.doc:
Group returns a grouped [slog.Attr] wrapped in a slice.
The group is constructed with the given key and the slice returned from
[Attrs] with the remaining arguments.

symbols.name: Handler
symbols.doc: Handler writes records to one writer.

symbols.name: HandlerOptions

symbols.name: Handlers

symbols.name: Info

symbols.doc: IsEnabledHandler is a predicate matching enabled [Handler]s.

symbols.name: IsHandler

symbols.name: IsTerminal

IsTerminal reports whether writer targets a terminal. A writer qualifies if
it implements TerminalWriter and reports true, or exposes an Fd that
term.IsTerminal recognizes.

symbols.name: IsTerminalHandler
symbols.doc: IsTerminalHandler is a predicate matching enabled terminal writers.

symbols.name: Level
symbols.doc:
Level is a log severity. Higher values are more verbose. The zero value is
invalid.

symbols.name: LevelRange
symbols.doc: LevelRange returns the lowest and highest valid levels.

symbols.name: Log
symbols.doc: Log calls Log on the package-level driver.

symbols.name: MapHandlers
symbols.doc: MapHandlers calls [Driver.MapHandlers] on the package-level driver.

symbols.name: New
symbols.doc:
New returns a Driver with one handler per option. It errors on an invalid
option.

symbols.name: RemoveHandlers
symbols.doc: RemoveHandlers calls [Driver.RemoveHandlers] on the package-level driver.

symbols.name: SetDefault
symbols.doc: SetDefault replaces the package driver. Nil installs a no-op driver.

symbols.name: TerminalWriter
symbols.doc:
TerminalWriter lets a writer declare itself a terminal, so callers can mark
in-memory writers as terminals for format and source defaults.

TestAppendJSONString_MatchesEncodingJSON locks the direct JSON string encoder
to byte-identical output with encoding/json's default (HTML-escaping) encoder.

symbols.name: appendJSONField
symbols.signature: func appendJSONField(buf *bytes.Buffer, first *bool, key, value string)

symbols.name: appendJSONKey
symbols.doc:
appendJSONKey writes a JSON object key using Go-syntax quoting, reusing the
buffer's spare capacity to avoid a per-key allocation.

symbols.name: appendJSONString
symbols.doc:
appendJSONString writes s as a quoted JSON string, matching encoding/json's
default (HTML-escaping) encoder byte-for-byte without allocating.

symbols.name: appendJSONValue
symbols.doc:
appendJSONValue writes value as a JSON token, encoding primitives directly to
avoid per-value reflection and allocation. Floats and arbitrary values fall
back to encoding/json to preserve its exact formatting.

symbols.name: errf
symbols.doc: errf is a helper that appends a formatted message to a wrapped error.
symbols.signature: func errf(err error, template string, args ...any) (error)

symbols.name: joinParts
symbols.doc:
joinParts returns a message builder that joins parts with a single space.
The builder is invoked lazily by emit only when a handler will receive the
record, avoiding the join cost for silenced records.

symbols.name: jsonSafe
symbols.doc:
jsonSafe reports whether the ASCII byte can be emitted in a JSON string
without escaping under encoding/json's default HTML-escaping policy.

symbols.name: reprVariant
symbols.doc:
reprVariant is a pair of representations of the same value:
a detailed, long format and a concise, short format.

symbols.name: sprintfMessage
symbols.doc:
sprintfMessage returns a message builder that formats args per format.
The builder is invoked lazily by emit only when a handler will receive the
record, avoiding the formatting cost for silenced records.

symbols.name: Author
symbols.doc: Author lists the project authors.

symbols.name: AuthorInfo
symbols.doc: AuthorInfo is an author's name and email.

symbols.name: CachePath
symbols.doc: CachePath joins elem onto the cache directory and returns an absolute path.

symbols.name: ConfigPath
symbols.doc: ConfigPath joins elem onto the config directory and returns an absolute path.

symbols.name: EntryPath
symbols.doc:
EntryPath returns the entry-point file path and true, or "" and false if
none is found.

symbols.name: Meta
symbols.doc: Meta holds version and license metadata set at startup.

symbols.name: globalEntryFile
symbols.doc: file name in XDG config directories

symbols.name: localEntryFile
symbols.doc: file name during CWD traversal

symbols.name: resolveEntryPath
symbols.signature: func resolveEntryPath(cwd, home string, cfgDirs []string) (string, bool)

symbols.name: rootPrefix
symbols.doc:
rootPrefix returns the physical root prefix of the system and a logical root
prefix specified by the environment variable [Name]_PREFIX, if set.

The physical root prefix is "/" on Unix and "<Drive>:" on Windows, where the
drive is determined by the current working directory.

The logical root prefix is used for resolving system paths such as
"[rootPrefix]/etc", "[rootPrefix]/tmp", and others, and defaults to the
physical root prefix if the environment variable is not set.

symbols.name: License
symbols.doc: License is the module's license text, embedded at build time.

symbols.name: Version
symbols.doc: Version is the module's semantic version, embedded at build time.

	{"uint", slog.Uint64("k", 7), `"k":7`, "attr.k=7"},
	{"float", slog.Float64("k", 3.5), `"k":3.5`, "attr.k=3.5"},
	{"duration", slog.Duration("k", dur), `"k":"1.5s"`, "attr.k=1.5s"},
	{"time", slog.Time("k", when), `"k":"` + when.Format(time.RFC3339Nano) + `"`, "attr.k=" + when.Format(time.RFC3339Nano)},
	{"string", slog.String("k", "a\"b"), `"k":"a\"b"`, `attr.k="a\"b"`},
	{"any", slog.Any("k", stringerValue{2}), `"k":{}`, "attr.k=S2"},
}
